baseline_gap: Verified % reduction not computed — a common baseline (original ShipShape) has not been independently counted. Recorded, not faked.

considered_methods.why: Counts `import { X as Y }` aliases as casts. Measured here: crude `as` ~956 vs real 284 for one repo — unusable for the `as` metric.
Self-reported, and `consistent-type-assertions` flags only a narrow subset of `as` usages — not the spec's 'type assertions (as)'.
Authoritative for cast/non-null/any nodes and implicit-any diagnostics (untyped params), consistent across repos, and safe because it only parses/typechecks — never executes the clone.

records.held_loosely: Count is syntactic/diagnostic — cannot judge whether a remaining cast is justified or a removed `any` was genuinely narrowed. Revisit with a type-aware pass or diff-based superficiality method.

any.note: self-report roughly consistent with verified count

as.note: self-report rejected — verified count diverges materially

nonnull.note: self-report roughly consistent with verified count

total.note: self-report rejected — verified count diverges materially
